steps.title: Find the verb tense relationship
steps.content: The main clause says Faure’s design increased the electricity the battery could hold — a past‑tense action. The relative clause “which Gaston Planté ______ fifteen years earlier” describes what happened to that battery before the increase. Therefore the verb in the blank must show an action completed earlier than the main clause’s past action.

steps.title: Choose the past perfect form
steps.content: Past perfect is formed with had + past participle (had invented), which exactly matches choice D. The other three options use present or future forms and do not show the earlier past action. That matches choice D — D is correct.
